programs.programName: Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), Qualified Individual (QI/SLMB-135)

verified.notes: QMB program has no age requirement (serves Medicare beneficiaries of all ages). Income and asset limits are 2026 federal limits from Medicare.gov. New Hampshire uses federal asset limits for QMB, SLMB, and QI programs. Phone number for New Hampshire MSP application not found in provided search results; contact your state Medicaid office or Medicare.gov for application assistance.

programs.notes: QMB program has no age requirement (serves Medicare beneficiaries of all ages). Income and asset limits are 2026 federal limits from Medicare.gov. Phone number for New Hampshire MSP application not found in provided search results; contact your state Medicaid office or Medicare.gov for application assistance.

verified.notes: Age threshold of 60+ qualifies under 'Households with Separate, Elderly, and Disabled Members' category with gross income limit of 165% FPL; however, general households with member 60+ can qualify under standard 200% FPL limit. Income limits shown are for standard 200% FPL pathway (current as of Oct 1, 2025 through Sept 30, 2026). Asset limits of $4,250 apply to households with at least one member age 60+ or with disability. Phone number is main NH DHHS SNAP line; alternative assistance line is 1-877-347-7627.

verified.notes: Adult Day Services in New Hampshire is a general program category rather than a single unified state program with centralized eligibility criteria. Search results describe adult day care as non-residential services for older adults and individuals with disabilities, but do not provide specific age minimums, income limits, asset tests, or a central application phone number. Individual programs (Easterseals, Senior Class, Silverthorne, etc.) operate independently with their own eligibility standards. To obtain accurate eligibility details, contact individual providers directly or consult the New Hampshire Department of Health and Human Services for Medicaid-funded adult day care specifics.

verified.notes: Age threshold varies by eligibility pathway: seniors must be 60+ to qualify as SERVICE RECIPIENTS (frail/homebound); volunteers must be 55+. Per user instructions to return senior-specific threshold, age 60 reflects the senior recipient requirement. Income limits ($25,760 single / $34,840 couple) apply to volunteers, not recipients. No asset limits found in authoritative sources. Phone number is Community Action Program Concord main line per Manchester NH official source.
